resources.string: Notifications for Fauxx background browsing diversification
Generating diverse browsing activity — %d actions today
Fauxx protects your privacy by generating synthetic browsing activity in the background. Here\'s what it does:
Performs web searches across Google, Bing, DuckDuckGo, and Yahoo on diverse topics
Visits a wide variety of websites to diversify your browsing profile
Generates DNS lookups for varied domains
All activity runs in the background and uses battery and data
Optionally, tell us about yourself on the next screens. This stays on your device and helps generate noise that\'s different from your real profile. Skip if you prefer uniform noise.
Fauxx will generate diverse browsing activity that\'s maximally different from your real behavioral signal. Toggle protection on from the Dashboard to start.
Helps us avoid generating activity that matches your demographic
Optional — used only to bias noise away from your demographic
We\'ll generate more noise in OTHER categories
Type specific interests not covered above (e.g., \"woodworking\", \"cryptocurrency\")
Used to compute demographic distance only
Used to generate searches and browsing activity suggesting other regions
Fauxx will perform the following background activities:
Search diverse topics on Google, Bing, DuckDuckGo, and Yahoo
Visit a variety of websites to broaden your browsing profile
Generate DNS lookups across varied domains
Use battery and mobile data while running in the background
All activity is synthetic and stays on your device. No personal data is collected or transmitted.
An open-source privacy tool that poisons data broker and ad-tech profiles by generating continuous, plausible, off-demographic synthetic activity from your device.
Data that stays on your device

All personal data remains exclusively on your device and is encrypted at rest:

• Demographic profile (age, gender, interests, profession, region)
• Ad platform profile cache (scraped interest categories)
• Synthetic persona history
• Action audit logs

This data is stored in an encrypted database (SQLCipher) with a key managed by Android Keystore. It never leaves your device in any form.

Fauxx generates synthetic browsing activity to confuse tracking profiles. This means the app will:

• Perform web searches on Google, Bing, DuckDuckGo, and Yahoo
• Visit categorized URLs to accumulate diverse tracker cookies
• Load diverse pages in background web views
• Resolve domain names to generate DNS query noise
• Open app store deep links for off-profile apps

All requests use randomized User-Agent headers and are rate-limited to avoid disrupting target services. Blocked domains (private networks, harmful content) are never contacted.

• No analytics or telemetry of any kind
• No crash reports sent to external services
• No advertising identifiers or tracking pixels
• No server-side accounts or cloud storage
• No data shared with third parties

Fauxx has no backend server. The app is entirely self-contained.

If enabled, the Location Signal module generates search queries and visits websites that suggest activity in regions different from your actual location, making it harder for data brokers to pinpoint where you live.

You can delete all stored data at any time via Settings > Clear All Data. This permanently removes your demographic profile, platform caches, persona history, action logs, and resets all settings to defaults. Uninstalling the app also removes all data.
Fauxx is open-source software. Source code and license details are available in the project repository.
If you find Fauxx useful, consider buying me a coffee.
To comply with Play Store policies, this build disables ad-profile poisoning and GPS location spoofing. For the full, unrestricted version of Fauxx, install from F-Droid or GitHub.
Synthetic activity will also use this language
Fauxx crashed during the last session. You can share the crash report to help diagnose the issue. The report has been scrubbed of personal data.
Why does Fauxx sometimes stop and show a “Tap to resume protection” notification?

Fauxx voluntarily pauses the engine when one of your configured constraints kicks in:

• Quiet hours: if you have an active-hours window set (default 7am–11pm), Fauxx stops at the end of the window and posts a one-tap notification scheduled for the next start time.
• WiFi only: if you have “WiFi only” enabled in Settings and lose WiFi for more than 30 minutes, Fauxx releases the foreground-service slot rather than spin idle.
• Battery threshold: if your battery is below the configured threshold and the device isn't charging, Fauxx pauses until the situation changes.

Tapping the notification opens Fauxx and restarts protection. Nothing is lost — your settings, profile, persona, and action log are persistent across pauses.

If you're seeing the engine stop outside of these configured constraints, please file an issue from Settings (“File a GitHub issue”) — that's a bug, not by design.
What does “Noise Ratio” on the Dashboard mean?

It's a throughput indicator: actions today divided by 500, capped at 100%. 500 actions in a day reads as a saturated 100%.

It does NOT measure the quality of the noise — whether the synthetic activity is hitting off-profile categories, fooling profiling systems, or covering a diverse range of topics. It's just a rate gauge.

For a view of where the noise is actually going, check the Targeting screen's category-weight chart. Red bars are categories Fauxx is suppressing (because they match your real profile), green bars are categories it's boosting, gray is neutral.
How does Layer 2 (Ad Profile Import) work, and where do I get the export file?

Layer 2 takes the ad-targeting profile that Google and Facebook already keep on you and uses it to steer Fauxx away from your real interests — synthetic activity gets boosted in the OTHER categories.

You export your own data and import the file into Fauxx; no sign-in inside the app, no automated scraping. Android sandboxes cookies per app, so Fauxx genuinely can't see your browser session, and Google + Facebook block sign-in from any embedded WebView, so a “sign in here” flow isn't possible. User-driven export sidesteps both.

How to export:

• Google — visit takeout.google.com in your browser, select “My Ad Center” specifically (skip the rest of the takeout to keep the file small), pick JSON format, request the export. Google emails a download link, usually within minutes. Tap “Import Google Takeout” in Fauxx and pick the resulting .zip (or the MyAdCenter.json file from inside it).
• Facebook — visit accountscenter.facebook.com, choose Download Your Information → JSON format → “Ads and businesses”. Facebook prepares the archive, emails the link, and you import it via “Import Facebook DYI”.

Things to know:

• Read-only. Fauxx never modifies or interacts with your ad settings on either platform — only reads what's in the export.
• Stays on device. The imported categories are written to the same SQLCipher-encrypted database as the rest of your data. Nothing leaves your phone.
• Refresh cadence is up to you. Re-import whenever you want a fresh snapshot — Fauxx will nudge you with an in-app banner if the last import is over 90 days old (snooze / mute available).
Fauxx disabled Layer 2 and the engine after repeated startup hangs. Re-enable them from Targeting and Dashboard when you\'re ready to try again.
Executes search queries across Google, Bing, DuckDuckGo, Yahoo
Visits 10,000+ URLs in background to accumulate diverse tracker cookies
Resolves diverse domains to generate ISP-visible DNS query noise
Rotates User-Agent, canvas noise, and navigator properties
Loads ad-heavy pages and visits ad preference dashboards
Feeds synthetic GPS routes via MockLocationProvider
Requires Developer Options enabled and Fauxx selected as mock location app
Opens app store pages for off-profile apps to trigger attribution pixels

Android requires you to designate Fauxx as the mock location app:

1. Enable Developer Options if you haven't yet: Settings → About phone → tap Build Number 7 times.

2. Open Developer Options → "Select mock location app" and choose Fauxx.

Without this step, Location Spoofing will silently do nothing.
Fauxx is registered as the system mock-location provider and feeding synthetic GPS fixes.
Fauxx is not the mock location app
Open Developer Options → Select mock location app → choose Fauxx, then restart the engine.
The system blocked addTestProvider despite the app op being allowed. Try toggling Fauxx off and back on under Developer Options → Select mock location app, then restart Fauxx.
Android refused the mock provider for an unexpected reason. Check app logs for details.
After a reboot, show a notification to resume protection. Android blocks apps from silently starting themselves in the background, so a tap is required.
Keep running below the threshold when the device is plugged in.
Activity is paused outside these hours
A narrow activity window (%dh) can itself be a trackable signal. Wider windows (12h+) are harder for trackers to distinguish from real usage.

By default, Fauxx rotates between many browser identifiers — but real people usually use one browser, so the variety can itself look like bot traffic. Tap below to use this device\'s built-in browser identifier instead, so the noise blends with your real activity. Leave blank to keep the default rotation.
Browser identifier (advanced — leave blank to auto-rotate)

This will permanently delete:
• All action logs
• Your demographic profile
• Ad platform profile cache
• Persona generation history

All settings will be reset to defaults. The engine will stop and return to Layer 0 (uniform noise).

This cannot be undone.
Boosts noise away from your declared demographics
Imports your existing ad profile from Google Takeout / Facebook DYI
Export your ad-targeting data from Google Takeout or Facebook Download Your Information, then import the file here. Fauxx never logs in for you and never modifies your ad settings.
Couldn\'t read the file. Permission may have been revoked.

Couldn\'t find an ad-interest file in the archive. Export \"My Ad Center\" specifically from Google Takeout. If you have personalized ads turned off, Google keeps no ad profile to export, so turn them on first to build one worth poisoning.
Couldn\'t find an ad-interest file inside the archive. From accountscenter.facebook.com, choose Download Your Information → JSON format → \"Ads and businesses\" specifically.
Your Layer 2 ad profile is over 90 days old. Re-import a fresh export to keep targeting up to date.
This will delete your demographic profile, all platform data, and persona history. The engine will revert to uniform random targeting.
No profile saved. Setting one up lets Layer 1 steer the noise away from your real demographics.
Add specific interests to suppress (mapped to nearest category)
Layer 2 couldn\'t read your ad profiles

The Adversarial Scraper tries to read what Google and Facebook think they know about you, then steers the noise away from those interests. It just got back an empty list, which usually means the scraper has no signed-in session for those sites.

Heads-up: Fauxx's scraper has its own browser session, separate from Chrome/Brave/Firefox where you may already be signed in — Android doesn't let apps share login cookies with each other. And Google/Facebook don't allow sign-in from an in-app browser either, so a 'sign in here' button isn't possible.

Layer 2 is being redesigned to import your ad profile directly (via Google Takeout or a browser bookmarklet) so it doesn't need a live session at all. In the meantime, Layers 1 and 3 still work without any scraping.
Notification permission denied — background activity indicator is hidden. Grant notification permission in Settings to see the status notification.
Android battery optimization is restricting Fauxx. Background activity will pause when the screen is off. Allow unrestricted background usage to keep the engine running.
Wi-Fi only mode — waiting for Wi-Fi

Shows how your synthetic noise is spread across topic categories. A wider spread means trackers see a more confused profile. Categories are weighted by the targeting engine to maximize distance from your real interests.

A synthetic identity the engine adopts for about a week. Activity is weighted toward this persona\'s interests to create temporally coherent noise — making it harder for trackers to filter out the fake signal. Personas rotate automatically.

Estimates how much of your visible browsing profile is synthetic noise vs real activity. Higher is better — at 80%%+, a data broker would need to correctly identify 4 out of 5 signals as fake to build an accurate profile.
Keep Fauxx running in the background
Android aggressively pauses background apps to save battery. For continuous profile poisoning, Fauxx needs to be exempt from battery optimization.
On the next screen, choose \"Allow\" so the engine can keep generating synthetic activity while your screen is off.
All personal data has been scrubbed from the logs.
Opens a pre-filled issue form. Logs are embedded directly when they fit, otherwise copied to your clipboard for pasting below.
Send to email, Slack, or another app
Crash report ready — review and submit
Truncated trace embedded; full trace on clipboard — paste below if you want the full context
No browser found — logs copied to clipboard
No browser found to open issue tracker
